text: The SCE-MI infrastructure shall provide multiple communication channels between software models and a device under test (DUT).
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall present channel end points as message ports to the DUT model.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall present channel end points as message port proxies to the software models.
The SCE-MI shall support both untimed and RTL modeling environments.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall provide message-oriented communication where a single message can trigger multiple clocked events in hardware.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall provide clock and reset generation to the hardware side.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall provide a service loop function to service communication channels.
The service loop function shall be capable of being called from a dedicated thread in multi-threaded environments.
The transactors shall decompose messages from input channels into sequences of cycle-accurate events for the DUT.
The transactors shall recompose sequences of events from the DUT into messages for output channels.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall allow any transactor to freeze controlled time by stopping the controlled clock.
The infrastructure linker shall analyze parameters from interface macros to establish the dimensions of the interface.
The software models shall bind to message port proxies using specific API calls.
The SceMiMessageInPort macro shall present messages arriving from the software side to the transactor.
The SceMiMessageOutPort macro shall allow transactors to send messages to the software side.
The SceMiClockPort macro shall supply a controlled clock and a controlled reset to the DUT.
The SceMiClockControl macro shall allow a transactor to control a DUT clock.
The hardware side message port macros shall use a dual-ready handshake protocol.
The SCE-MI software side shall provide a C++ API for infrastructure access.
The SCE-MI software side shall provide an ANSI standard C API.
The software side shall provide an error handling facility for irrecoverable errors via a registered error handler.
The SceMiMessageData object shall represent the vector of message data transferred between software and hardware.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall support cycle stamping for output messages sent to the software side.
The infrastructure shall notify the software side when the hardware is ready for more input if an input ready callback is registered.
The controlled reset shall remain asserted for a minimum duration specified by the ResetCycles parameter.
The SceMiParameters class shall provide an API to access the interface parameter set.
The SCE-MI infrastructure shall delete objects constructed by the API during shutdown.
The infrastructure linker shall uniquely distinguish transactor instances by their hierarchical path names.
The SceMiClockPort macro shall support user-defined clock ratios relative to the highest frequency controlled clock.
The SceMiClockControl macro shall provide signals to indicate when a controlled clock edge occurs on the next uclock posedge.
